name: Mohammad Shazzad Hossain, PhD
title: Associate Professor
bio_raw:
Dr Mohammad Shazzad Hossain is an e-learning expert, Director of the Centre for Excellence in Teaching and Learning, and Associate Professor in the Department of Media Studies and Journalism at the University of Liberal Arts Bangladesh. Since 2012, he has helped initiate innovative teaching techniques and vibrant cocurricular activities in the Department. In recognition of his contributions, the university honoured him with the Vice Chancellor Award of Excellence in 2017.
He has a PhD in E-learning Methodology and expertise in film studies, journalism, community media, new media, and development communication. As an expert, he has collaborated with numerous government and development agencies on projects such as e-learning module design, documentary production, research, and community development. Prior to joining academia, he worked as a media professional for eight years. His interdisciplinary knowledge and skills make him a sought-after expert in both academic and industrial settings.
stated_interests: Higher Education, E-learning, Pedagogy, AI in Education, AI in Journalism and Communication, Community Media, New Media, Gender, Film Studies, Photography

Awards and Recognition
2025:    Best paper award for the paper titled ‘Exploring E-Readiness in Online Filmmaking Education in Bangladesh’ in the category Curriculum Innovation and Innovative Assessment Methods in the
Educational Innovation Symposium 2025
, Dhaka.
2025:    Served as a member of the Judging Panel (second round) for the 2024
QS Reimagine Education Awards
, London.
2024:    Nominated as Academic Advisor, ERASMUS+ project Adapting Climate Change Education, Skills and Sustainability for Advancing Locally-Led Solutions (ACCESS4ALL) by
BRAC James P Grant School of Public Health and EU
under the ERASMUS+ Capacity Building for Higher Education (CBHE) program.
2023:    Served as a pedagogical expert reviewing Digital Technology textbooks for grades 6-10, developed by the
National Curriculum and Textbook Board (NCTB)
under the National Curriculum 2021.
2023:    Developed ten e-learning course modules as a subject matter expert for the DIKKHA Project, under the
Bangladesh Government's ICT Division
.
2023:    Served as a member of the Judging Panel for the 2023
QS Reimagine Education Awards
, Abu Dhabi.
2017:
Vice Chancellor Award of Excellence
for Community Engagement. Awarded by the University of Liberal Arts Bangladesh.
2017:    Documentary film ‘Behind a Wall of Justice’ has been selected for the
3rd South Asian International Documentary festival (SAID)
from Feb 17th -19th, 2017, in Seattle, USA.
2015:    Research proposal ‘Giving Voice to the Voiceless: Community Radio in Bangladesh’ wins the
research grant
in the second round of the ULAB research programme.
2009:    Documentary film ‘Japeto Jebon’ wins second prize in “Bangladesh Documentary Festival 2009” by
Bangladesh Documentary Council
.
2001:    Black & white photograph ‘Sorger Hasi’ wins first prize in “5th Photography exhibition” by
Shahjalal University Photographers’ Association (SUPA)
.
